name: La Sagra – circular from Cedillo del Condado through Yunclillos and Chozas
name_original: La Sagra – circular desde Cedillo del Condado por Yunclillos y Chozas
region_intro: Road cycling around Cedillo Del Condado is characterized by the expansive Manchega plain, offering a landscape of flat to gently rolling terrain. Routes often traverse rural paths and connect various towns, providing a mix of open spaces and semi-urban scenery. Cyclists can encounter small bodies of water in old quarries, adding visual interest to the consistent landscape. The region provides a network of routes suitable for different skill levels, from easy rides to more demanding distances. Best road cycling routes around Cedillo Del Condado The most popular road cycling route is La Sagra – circular from Illescas via Cedillo and Griñón, a 37.5 miles (60.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey connecting several towns across the plain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Sagra – circular from Cedillo del Condado through Yunclillos and Chozas, a moderate 32.2 miles (51.9 km) path. This circular route explores the rural landscape around Cedillo del Condado. Local road cyclists also love the Illescas, Griñón, Par, a 50.4 miles (81.1 km) trail leading through a series of towns, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Cedillo Del Condado is defined by the open Manchega plain, rural roads, and connections between small towns. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Cedillo Del Condado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cedillo Del Condado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Cedillo del Condado?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Cedillo del Condado, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Cedillo del Condado?
A: The region is characterized by the expansive Manchega plain, meaning you'll primarily encounter flat to gently rolling terrain. Routes often traverse rural paths and connect various towns, providing a mix of open spaces and semi-urban scenery. You might also spot small bodies of water in old quarries, adding visual interest.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cedillo del Condado?
A: Yes, Cedillo del Condado offers 13 easy road cycling routes. These are perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride, allowing you to enjoy the landscape without significant challenges.

Q: What about more challenging road cycling routes?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there is at least one difficult road cycling route available. Additionally, 16 moderate routes offer longer distances and more elevation gain, providing a good workout for those looking to push themselves.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular journeys. For example, you can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027001795/la-sagra-circular-from-illescas-via-cedillo-and-grinon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sagra – circular from Illescas via Cedillo and Griñón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 60.3 km route, or the 51.9 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027099999/la-sagra-circular-from-cedillo-del-condado-through-yunclillos-and-chozas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sagra – circular from Cedillo del Condado through Yunclillos and Chozas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic points or attractions I can see along the routes?
A: While cycling, you can pass through or near several interesting spots. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Illescas&lt;/a&gt;, or the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chozas de Canales&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer views of the historic city of Toledo in the distance.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling experience here?
A: The road cycling routes around Cedillo del Condado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 19 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain and the opportunity to connect different towns across the plain.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Cedillo del Condado?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in this region, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, especially in the open plains, while winters are typically cool but often dry enough for riding.

Q: Are there routes that pass through multiple towns?
A: Yes, many routes are designed to connect various towns, offering a mix of rural and semi-urban scenery. A notable example is the 81.1 km route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977181765/illescas-grinon-par" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Illescas, Griñón, Par&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through a series of settlements.

Q: What is the typical distance for road cycling routes in Cedillo del Condado?
A: The routes vary significantly in length. You can find shorter rides around 30-40 km, while longer routes can extend to over 80 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Segovia – Plaza Mayor of Navalcarnero loop from Illescas&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 78 km.

Q: Are there any less crowded road cycling options?
A: Given the extensive network of routes across the Manchega plain, it's possible to find less crowded options, especially on weekdays or by exploring some of the lesser-known rural paths. The region offers enough variety to spread out cyclists.
tips: Its name may be a simple diminutive of Yuncos, which in turn is the name of a nearby town, but in 1161 it appears in Mozarabic documents with the name "Onclelos", which could be a diminutive of a source that later derived in your current spelling.

If you are interested in the phenomena of real estate speculation, you can take a ride with your bike through the successive extensions that were made in this agricultural town in the 2000s, when it was intended to turn it into a new "dormitory city" for Madrid. The town went from having 500 to 2,000 homes, many of which were never inhabited, or even completed. The town went through a tough time after the real estate crisis with high unemployment rates and the hangover of this recent history still lingers today.

A curiosity of this town is that its origin is not known with certainty. It is known that pre-Roman tribes already inhabited it and then it passed through Roman, Arab hands and, after the conquest of Toledo by Alfonso VI in 1086, Mozarabs and finally Christians.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of St. John the Baptist, Cedillo del Condado
